user_request: Draft a suit for recovery of Rs.20,00,000/- paid as advance for a business transaction which failed due to Defendant's default. Plead total failure of consideration under Section 65 of Indian Contract Act. Claim refund with interest and costs. Include cause of action paragraph with accrual date, continuing nature of cause of action, valuation, court fee, and proper verification clause. Draft suitable for filing before the District Court.

facts.summary: Plaintiff seeks recovery of Rs. 20,00,000 paid as advance for a business transaction that failed due to the Defendant's default. The suit claims total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act and seeks a refund with interest and costs.

assumptions: Jurisdiction lies in the District Court based on valuation or cause of action
Interest rate will be determined by court or statutory rates if not specified

possession, is not maintainable. Thus, where an application to amend the plaint to convert it from a simple suit of 
injunction to one for recovery of possession, was filed after nine years of the filing of the suit for injunction, the 
application was held to be hopelessly barred by limitation and was held not maintainable.43

filled_sections.jurisdiction:
1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.
2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the Defendant resides at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, which falls within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the cause of action arose within such territorial limits as the {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} was entered into at {{TRANSACTION_PLACE}} within the jurisdiction of this Court.
3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.
filled_sections.facts:
4. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and has been engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}} for the past several years.
5. The Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} and is known to the Plaintiff through {{RELATIONSHIP_CONTEXT}}.
6. On or about {{TRANSACTION_DATE}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and represented that {{DEFENDANT_REPRESENTATION}}. Based on the said representations, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} for which the Defendant sought an advance of Rs. 20,00,000.
7. In pursuance of the said arrangement and in good faith reliance upon the Defendant's representations, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000 to the Defendant on {{PAYMENT_DATE}} through {{PAYMENT_MODE}}. Proof of payment is annexed as Annexure B.
8. The Defendant undertook to complete the contemplated transaction or to provide goods/services in exchange for the advance by {{OBLIGATION_DUE_DATE}} and otherwise guaranteed performance; the Plaintiff performed his part by making the payment and by providing any necessary support for the transaction.
9. Despite Plaintiff's full performance, the Defendant has wholly failed, neglected and refused to {{BREACH_DESCRIPTION}} on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The Defendant's failure constitutes total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
10. Thereafter the Plaintiff made several oral requests and sent written reminders to the Defendant on {{FOLLOWUP_DATES}} urging refund of the amount. Copies of reminders are annexed as Annexure C.
11. The Plaintiff, through advocate, served a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} upon the Defendant demanding refund of Rs. 20,00,000 within 15 days (a true copy is annexed as Annexure D).
12. The Defendant did not comply with the demands; the Defendant's reply dated {{REPLY_DATE}}, if any, is evasive and does not address the substantive claims (Annexure E). Alternatively, no reply was issued within the stipulated period, thereby evidencing a clear intention not to honour the obligation.
13. As a direct consequence of the Defendant's default, the Plaintiff has suffered the following losses: (a) principal amount of Rs. 20,00,000; (b) loss of opportunity to deploy the said amount in business at prevailing rates; (c) mental agony, harassment and inconvenience. The Plaintiff has no alternative but to approach this Hon'ble Court for the reliefs sought herein.
filled_sections.legal_basis:
14. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 inasmuch as money was paid in pursuance of an existing consideration which thereafter fails, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to restitution of the amount paid. The facts show that Rs. 20,00,000 was paid for a contemplated business transaction, which has wholly fallen through due to the Defendant's default; the consideration has entirely failed, rendering the Plaintiff entitled to refund.
15. In the alternative, the Plaintiff submits that no part-performance by the Defendant justifies retention of the amount, and there is no lawful set-off or counter-claim against the Plaintiff in respect of the subject matter of the suit.
16. It is further submitted that the Plaintiff is entitled to such other relief as this Court may deem fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.
filled_sections.cause_of_action:
17. Origin: The cause of action first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant failed and refused to {{BREACH_EVENT}}, despite the Plaintiff having duly performed his part of the obligation under the {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}}.
18. Further accrual: The cause of action further arose on {{NOTICE_DATE}} when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed to comply with the demands therein and to refund the amount.
19. Continuing nature: The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to retain the Plaintiff's amount of Rs. 20,00,000 without lawful authority, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use of funds, and the said cause of action continues to accrue day by day within the jurisdiction of this Court.
filled_sections.limitation: 20. Limitation: Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963; For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails; Period: Three years. The suit is filed within the period from the date of payment, i.e., {{PAYMENT_DATE}}, and the cause first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The present suit is within the period of limitation.
filled_sections.valuation_court_fee: 21. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fee. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has to be paid on this plaint in accordance with the applicable Court Fees Act, computed on the suit valuation at the applicable ad valorem rate.
filled_sections.interest:
22. Interest: The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows, the Defendant having wrongfully retained the Plaintiff's funds and deprived the Plaintiff of the commercial use of the said amount:
    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{PAYMENT_DATE}} till the filing of this suit, amounting to approximately Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}. The rate is justified given the commercial nature of the transaction and the Plaintiff's loss of use of capital; prevailing market rates and the absence of any contractual stipulation limiting interest.
    (b) Pendente lite interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum during the pendency of this suit under Section 34 of the CPC, or at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable.
    (c) Future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation.
  The rate is chosen to reflect the commercial opportunity cost and to compensate the Plaintiff for the deprivation of use of his funds.

15. It is further submitted that the Plaintiff is entitled to such other relief as this Court may deem fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.


CAUSE OF ACTION

16. Origin: The cause of action first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}} when the Defendant failed and refused to {{BREACH_EVENT}}, despite the Plaintiff having duly performed his part of the obligation under the {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}}.
17. Further accrual: The cause of action further arose on {{NOTICE_DATE}} when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed to comply with the demands therein and to refund the amount.
18. Continuing nature: The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to retain the Plaintiff's amount of Rs. 20,00,000 without lawful authority, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use of funds, and the said cause of action continues to accrue day by day within the jurisdiction of this Court.


LIMITATION

19. Limitation: Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963; For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails; Period: Three years. The suit is filed within the period from the date of payment, i.e., {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, and the cause first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The present suit is within the period of limitation.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fee. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has to be paid on this plaint in accordance with the applicable Court Fees Act, computed on the suit valuation at the applicable ad valorem rate.


INTEREST

21. Interest: The Plaintiff claims interest on the suit amount as follows, the Defendant having wrongfully retained the Plaintiff's funds and deprived the Plaintiff of the commercial use of the said amount:
    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till the filing of this suit, amounting to approximately Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}. The rate is justified given the commercial nature of the transaction and the Plaintiff's loss of use of capital; prevailing market rates and the absence of any contractual stipulation limiting interest.
    (b) Pendente lite interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum during the pendency of this suit under Section 34 of the CPC, or at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable.
    (c) Future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation.
  The rate is chosen to reflect the commercial opportunity cost and to compensate the Plaintiff for the deprivation of use of his funds.


PRAYER

22. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only);
    (b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the said sum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till the date of filing of this suit;
    (c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the CPC at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable during the pendency of this suit;
    (d) Award future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till the date of actual realization;
    (e) Award costs of this suit including court fees and other pre-litigation costs incurred for issuing the legal notice;
    (f) Grant such other and further relief as this Hon'ble Court may deem fit and proper in the facts and circumstances of the case.

23. | Primary agreement/transaction document | Annexure A
24. | Proof of payment/performance by Plaintiff | Annexure B
25. | Correspondence/reminders sent to Defendant | Annexure C
26. | Legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} with postal receipt and AD card | Annexure D
27. | Defendant's reply to legal notice / proof of non-reply | Annexure E


VERIFICATION

Verified at {{COURT_PLACE}} on this the ____ day of __________ 20____.

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}

PLAINTIFF

{{ADVOCATE_NAME}}
Advocate

IN THE COURT OF THE DISTRICT JUDGE AT {{PLACE}}

O.S. No. __________ / 20____
Suit for recovery of Rs. 20,00,000/- with interest and costs under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872

{{PLAINTIFF_NAME}}, aged about {{PLAINTIFF_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}}. ... PLAINTIFF
{{DEFENDANT_NAME}}, aged about {{DEFENDANT_AGE}} years, Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}}, Residing at: {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}. ... DEFENDANT


JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.
2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the Defendant resides at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}}, which falls within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court, and the cause of action arose within such territorial limits as the {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} was entered into at {{TRANSACTION_PLACE}} within the jurisdiction of this Court.
3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court. This Court has subject matter jurisdiction to entertain suits of this nature.


FACTS OF THE CASE

4. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{PLAINTIFF_ADDRESS}} and has been engaged in {{PLAINTIFF_BUSINESS_DESCRIPTION}} for the past several yea{{AMOUNT}}. The Defendant is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} residing at {{DEFENDANT_ADDRESS}} and is known to the Plaintiff through {{RELATIONSHIP_CONTEXT}}.
5. On or about {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff and represented that {{DEFENDANT_REPRESENTATION}}. Based on the said representations, the Plaintiff and the Defendant entered into {{TRANSACTION_DESCRIPTION}} for which the Defendant sought an advance of Rs. 20,00,000.
6. In pursuance of the said arrangement and in good faith reliance upon the Defendant's representations, the Plaintiff paid Rs. 20,00,000 to the Defendant on {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} through {{PAYMENT_MODE}}. Proof of payment is annexed as Annexure B.
7. The Defendant undertook to complete the contemplated transaction or to provide goods/services in exchange for the advance by {{OBLIGATION_DUE_DATE}} and otherwise guaranteed performance; the Plaintiff performed his part by making the payment and by providing any necessary support for the transaction.
8. Despite Plaintiff's full performance, the Defendant has wholly failed, neglected and refused to {{BREACH_DESCRIPTION}} on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The Defendant's failure constitutes total failure of consideration under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.
9. Thereafter the Plaintiff made several oral requests and sent written reminders to the Defendant on {{FOLLOWUP_DATES}} urging refund of the amount. Copies of reminders are annexed as Annexure C.
10. The Plaintiff, through advocate, served a legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}} upon the Defendant demanding refund of Rs. 20,00,000 within 15 days (a true copy is annexed as Annexure D).
11. The Defendant did not comply with the demands; the Defendant's reply dated {{REPLY_DATE}}, if any, is evasive and does not address the substantive claims (Annexure E). Alternatively, no reply was issued within the stipulated period, thereby evidencing a clear intention not to honour the obligation.
12. As a direct consequence of the Defendant's default, the Plaintiff has suffered the following losses: (a) principal amount of Rs. 20,00,000; (b) loss of opportunity to deploy the said amount in business at prevailing rates; (c) mental agony, harassment and inconvenience. The Plaintiff has no alternative but to approach this Hon'ble Court for the reliefs sought herein.


LEGAL BASIS

13. The Plaintiff submits that the Defendant is liable under Section 65 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872 inasmuch as money was paid in pursuance of an existing consideration which thereafter fails, thereby entitling the Plaintiff to restitution of the amount paid. The facts show that Rs. 20,00,000 was paid for a contemplated business transaction, which has wholly fallen through due to the Defendant's default; the consideration has entirely failed, rendering the Plaintiff entitled to refund.
14. In the alternative, the Plaintiff submits that no part-performance by the Defendant justifies retention of the amount, and there is no lawful set-off or counter-claim against the Plaintiff in respect of the subject matter of the suit.
15. It is further submitted that the Plaintiff is entitled to such other relief as this Court may deem fit in the facts and circumstances of the case.

17. Further accrual: The cause of action further arose on {{NOTICE_DATE}} when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed to comply with the demands therein and to refund the amount.
18. Continuing nature: The cause of action is a continuing one as the Defendant continues to retain the Plaintiff's amount of Rs. 20,00,000 without lawful authority, thereby depriving the Plaintiff of the use of funds, and the said cause of action continues to accrue day by day within the jurisdiction of this Court.

19. Limitation: Article 47 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963; For money paid upon an existing consideration which afterwards fails; Period: Three years. The suit is filed within the period from the date of payment, i.e., {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}}, and the cause first arose on {{DEFAULT_DATE}}. The present suit is within the period of limitation.


VALUATION AND COURT FEE

20. The suit is valued at Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court-fee. The requisite court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has to be paid on this plaint in accordance with the applicable Court Fees Act, computed on the suit valuation at the applicable ad valorem rate.

    (a) Pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till the filing of this suit, amounting to approximately Rs. {{PRE_SUIT_INTEREST_AMOUNT}}. The rate is justified given the commercial nature of the transaction and the Plaintiff's loss of use of capital; prevailing market rates and the absence of any contractual stipulation limiting interest.
    (b) Pendente lite interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum during the pendency of this suit under Section 34 of the CPC, or at such rate as this Hon'ble Court may deem just and reasonable.
    (c) Future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till realisation.
  The rate is chosen to reflect the commercial opportunity cost and to compensate the Plaintiff for the deprivation of use of his funds.


PRAYER

22. In the premises stated hereinabove, it is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court may be graciously pleased to:
    (a) Pass a decree in favour of the Plaintiff and against the Defendant for Rs. 20,00,000/- (Rupees Twenty Lakh Only);
    (b) Award pre-suit interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum on the said sum from {{ADVANCE_PAYMENT_DATE}} till the date of filing of this suit;
    (c) Award pendente lite interest under Section 34 of the CPC at such rate as this Hon'ble Court deems just and reasonable during the pendency of this suit;
    (d) Award future interest at the rate of {{INTEREST_RATE}}% per annum from the date of decree till the date of actual realization;
